items.headline: City launching business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: Cupertino is offering an amnesty program to help businesses get proper licenses, targeting shopping centers where unlicensed operations may exist. This is part of a broader economic development push to formalize the informal business sector and support retail growth in the city.

items.headline: New incentives coming for retail, small business, and startup growth
items.summary: The city council is considering economic development incentives designed to attract and support retail businesses, small businesses, and startups in Cupertino. This suggests the city is trying to diversify its economy beyond tech.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing project approved at East Middlefield Road
items.summary: The city council gave final approval (second reading) to a mixed-use residential development at 490 East Middlefield Road. This project will add housing to the area, though specific unit counts and affordability details were not provided in the agenda.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive grant funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application to California's Prohousing Incentive Program (Round 4) seeking state funding to support housing development. If awarded, this could help finance future residential projects in Mountain View.

items.headline: City seeks public input on $1M+ federal housing grants
items.summary: Santa Clara is holding a public hearing on March 24 about how to spend HUD federal housing and urban development grant funds for 2026-27. Residents can comment on priorities like affordable housing, homelessness prevention, or other housing needs.

items.headline: City holding community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale is hosting a public meeting on Monday, March 23 at 5:30 p.m. to discuss the safe parking program, which typically provides designated lots where people experiencing homelessness can park overnight safely. This appears to be a chance for residents to learn about or weigh in on how the program operates.
